steps.title: Make a two-point table
steps.content: The starting wage is 10.40 dollars at $x=0$. After 5 yearly increases of 0.60 dollars, the wage is $10.40+5(0.60)=13.40$ dollars at $x=5$. These points confirm a slope of $0.60$ and an intercept of $10.40$.

steps.title: Use the slope and intercept
steps.content: The model starts at $10.40$ and adds $0.60$ for each year after January 1, 2018. Therefore $$f(x)=10.40+0.60x.$$

steps.title: Eliminate the traps
steps.content: $f(x) = 10.40 - 0.60x$ has the right starting value but a negative slope, so it models a wage decrease. Two other options make $10.40$ the yearly increase instead of the starting wage. $f(x) = 10.40 + 0.60x$ is the only model with intercept $10.40$ and slope $0.60$, so C is correct.
